author: han0110
content:
1. `POST /v1/execution_proof_requests` (proof request)

  Previously the body is SSZ encoded `NewPayloadRequest`, but since the stateless validator guest requires the activated fork and the activation condition as input, which depends on the genesis file, so I think it'd be more straightforward if the value comes from the CL (and zkboost can be less network setting aware).

  So I'd like to update the request body to a structure containing `NewPayloadRequest` and the [`ChainConfig`](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/tests-zkevm%40v0.4.1/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/stateless.py#L176), using SSZ encoding.

2.`POST /v1/execution_proof_verifications` (proof verification)

  Previously the body is raw proof bytes, and `new_payload_request_root` is attached as query param, but the `ChainConfig` is also a public values now, so same reason as above, it seems more robust if the CL can specify the value.

  So I'd like to update the request body a structure containing proof bytes and `new_payload_request_root`, `proof_type`, and the `ChainConfig`, using SSZ encoding.

If these make sense, I'd apply the changes in the next zkboost release.

author: taulepton_
content: This sounds reasonable to me, when you’ve made the changes if you can shoot over a link to the api documentation then I will make the changes to consensus specs to mirror
